title: Beesha capitata
taxonomy: Beesha capitata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of shrub that is commonly found in various parts of the world. Further classification of Beesha capitata includes its order as Asterales and its class as Magnoliopsida. Within the kingdom Plantae, Beesha capitata is a member of the phylum Magnoliophyta. Beesha capitata's exact classification might be subject to change as new research emerges.

intro: Beesha capitata, also known as Acmadenia tenax, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate conditions such as skin infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Beesha capitata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and limonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Beesha capitata is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
